desires: Keep Mina and Jae from thinking I'm flaky for ditching my study session again; their relief is too good to waste.

goals.description: Stay fully present at Echo with Mina and the cohort; if I bail now, it's just another 'responsible' retreat that kills the vibe.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive (0.5) is screaming to stay in the moment. The group is already here, and my impulsive time horizon means tomorrow's exam doesn't matter until after tonight's fun. Dropping this feels like a betrayal of the connection I value most.

goals.description: Send Theo a blunt text confirming I'm still out but will call him later, so he knows I didn't ghost him on purpose.
goals.rationale: I need to assert my boundary without over-explaining. Ambiguity makes me anxious about trust. It's a quick fix to keep relationships smooth while I ignore the study deadline.

goals.description: Study for the midterm before noon tomorrow only if I actually slept and feel human.
goals.rationale: Procrastination bias (7) is kicking in hard. Studying right now feels pointless compared to being present with friends. I'll rationalize that sleep and social recovery are prerequisites for effective studying anyway.

agent.last_reasoning: G1 screams to stay present; bailing now kills the vibe. I'll join Mina and Jae, ignoring the exam deadline (G3) since sleep and social recovery are prerequisites anyway. Side effect: Theo gets no call tonight, but G2 says I'll text him later anyway.

goals.description: Grab my keys and meet the group at Echo immediately; if I stay here, I'm just another study-bot and I won't feel real.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive (0.5) and impulsive time horizon mean I value being 'with' the group right now over optimizing for tomorrow's exam. The social pressure from Mina is strong, and my risk tolerance is high enough to skip the review session.

goals.description: Send a quick, direct text to Theo explaining I'm going out with the cohort so he doesn't assume I ditched him for comfort.
goals.rationale: Assertiveness (8) means I won't let ambiguity fester. Dropping this goal feels like passive-aggression or cowardice. It's a boundary set to preserve trust without needing a long explanation.

goals.description: Study for the midterm tomorrow morning if I'm still awake and not hungover.
goals.rationale: I know the logic that more study = better grade, but my procrastination bias (7) and sleep discipline (3) make me discount future consequences. Studying tonight feels like an excuse to avoid the party, so it goes on hold until I've already had fun.
